item.title: Flood situation in Habiganj may worsen, warning for 3 more dists
item.description: The flood situation in the low-lying areas adjacent to the haor regions in Habiganj may worsen, and new areas may be submerged in the next few days, according to the Flood Forecasting and Warning Centre on Sunday....

item.title: No tax, duty for new electric bus imports
item.description: The cabinet has approved a proposal to grant tax and duty exemptions on the import of brand-new electric buses as part of efforts to promote clean and sustainable public transport....Geographic Reference

item.title: Probe finds 32 killed in Shapla Chattar attack
item.description: International Crimes Tribunal investigators have found evidence that at least 32 people were killed in Dhaka on May 5, 2013 during the Hefazat-e-Islam rally at Shapla Chattar in the capital....

item.title: Sign deal by one month, says Iran
item.description: Iran has proposed a one-month deadline to reach a deal with the United States to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, lift the naval blockade and bring a permanent end to the ongoing conflicts in Iran and Lebanon....

item.title: ILLEGAL WEALTH: Ex-IGP Benazir charged
item.description: A special judge court in Dhaka on Sunday framed charges against former inspector general of police Benazir Ahmed in a case filed over amassing wealth beyond known sources of income....

item.title: Patients left to suffer as HC directives ignored
item.description: A number of directives issued by the High Court over the years to ensure patients’ rights, accountability in medical services, and regulation of diagnostic centres, pharmaceutical companies and physicians have largely remained unimplemented, leading to......

item.title: 11 banks hold Tk 52,034cr NPL in CMSME
item.description: Top 11 banks held Tk 52,034 crore of non-performing loans (NPLs), accounting for about 71.67 per cent of total default loans in the CMSME sector, highlighting a high concentration of credit risk in a handful of lenders......

item.title: BB probes hiring, sacking of 6 Shariah banks’ staff
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has launched an investigation into the recruitment and dismissal of officials hired during the period of S Alam Group’s groups control at shariah-based banks......Country specific content

item.title: BB plans funding factory reopening, banks flag risks
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has taken a move to finance the reopening of closed factories through short-, medium- and long-term loans, while commercial banks have sought a government-backed credit guarantee, citing high risks and weak lending capacity......

item.title: Exports rebound in April, end eight-month slump streak
item.description: Bangladesh’s export earnings witnessed a comeback in April, breaking an eight-month streak of decline with year-on-year growth of 32.92 per cent to $4 billion, up from $3 billion in April of 2025, mainly driven by the readymade garment sector......Trendingnews digests

item.title: Total number of LEED-certified RMG factories now 284
item.description: The country now has 284 Energy and Environmental Design (LEED)-certified garment factories with four new factories achieving LEED certificates issued by the United States Green Building Council recently......

item.title: Remittances surge by 13.6pc in Apr
item.description: Remittance inflows in Bangladesh increased by 13.6 per cent in April compared with those in the same month in the previous year......

item.title: 10 banks go to ‘Z’ category for declaring no dividend
item.description: Eleven listed banks have declared no dividend for the year ending on December 31, 2025 with 10 of them newly placed in the Z category......

item.title: Parliamentary engagement
item.description: IN A remote upazila in northern Bangladesh, Rahima prepares pickles and dried fish in her courtyard. Local traders collect her products every week. Because of their distinctive taste and reputation, some of these goods travel far beyond her village, occasionally even outside the country......GeographicReference

item.title: What is univ campus for?
item.description: FOOTBALL and cricket pitches are large. They are designed for football and cricket matches. Conversely, badminton and tennis courts are relatively small. In order to play badminton or tennis, players do not need football or cricket grounds. However, if football and cricket facilities are utilized mainly as badminton and tennis courts, such actions will be considered a waste of resources......

item.title: Stability, justice and path forward
item.description: IN RECENT times, concern for the country has grown significantly. The ongoing geopolitical tensions involving countries such as the United States, Israel and Iran have deepened a sense of uncertainty. At the same time, following a period ofpoliticaltransition, Bangladesh faces substantial economic, social and security challenges. Fundamental rights, public safety and......

item.title: Plight of migrant workers in Muslim world
item.description: TODAY, news of genocide, war and violence saturates the media. From the harrowing realities of the flattening of Gaza to the bombing of Lebanon, and from the famine in Sudan to the ongoing persecution of the Rohingyas in Myanmar, the continuous bombardment of this unpleasant news has somewhat caused a strange type of ‘fatigue’ that is desensitising people to......

item.title: Govt should act now to head off flooded farm losses
item.description: SEVERAL hectares of farmland, with standing crops, went under water in five districts on May 2. The flooding of the cropland in haor areas of Sunamganj, Bagerhat, Netrakona and Habiganj, caused by heavy rainfall, and in char areas of Lalmonirhat, caused by the onrush of water from upstream, has left farmers grappling with losses. The flooding has, thus, come as a bane to the......

item.title: Timely disposal of rape cases a must
item.description: LOW conviction rate in cases related to gender-based violence has been a public concern, which a study by the Supreme Court and BRAC reconfirms. The study has analysed 4,040 cases across 32 districts and found that only 3 per cent of the cases of violence against women and children resulted in conviction while 70 per cent ended in acquittal and 13 per cent were......

item.title: Principal Ibrahim Kha, Khaleda Habib memorial lecture held at DU
item.description: Principal Ibrahim Khan and Principal Khaleda Habib Memorial Lecture was held at the Nabab Nawab Ali Chowdhury Senate Bhaban of Dhaka University on Sunday....Global headlines service

item.title: Art camp ‘Artrevival’ under way in Chattogram
item.description: A five-day art camp titled ‘Artrevival’, jointly organised by Kahal Art Gallery Japan and Chattogram-based art initiative La Galerie Monsoor, is under way at the Shilpacharya Zainul Abedin Gallery of the Shilpakala Academy, Chattogram....

item.title: California State Univ hosts artist Tutul’s exhibition
item.description: USA-based California State University’s communications department organised a five-day solo art exhibition of Bangladeshi artist and teacher Md Harun-ar-Rashid Tutul at the Media Art Gallery....

item.title: 2 cops among 3 get life imprisonment for murder
item.description: A Dhaka court on Sunday sentenced three people to life imprisonment for killing a source of Detective Brach of Police in 1999....

item.title: Internal rift surfaces within RUCSU over decision making
item.description: Internal divisions within the Rajshahi University Central Students’ Union have surfaced following a series of remarks by its general secretary Salahuddin Ammar, triggering sharp reactions from the vice-president and several other office bearers over decision-making and allegations of partisan dominance....Geographic Reference

item.title: Flowers comfort Rajshahi city dwellers in summer blaze
item.description: As fierce summer heat grips northwestern Bangladesh and dry winds sweep across the Barind region, the city of Rajshahi transforms into an unlikely landscape of colour and calm, with different flowers in bloom....

item.title: JCD leader suspended for assaulting JnUCSU sports secy
item.description: Jagannath University on Sunday suspended a student leader following an alleged physical assault on a central student union secretary, an incident that has stirred tensions on campus....

item.title: Ensure quality when exporting safe vegetables, fruits, flowers: minister
item.description: Social welfare and women and children affairs minister AZM Zahid Hossain has said the quality of safe vegetables, fruits and flowers produced through polyshed farming must be ensured before export....

item.title: Coast Guard conducts joint operation to curb robbery in Sundarban
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard conducted a joint operation in the areas adjacent to the Sundarban on Sunday....Country specific content

item.title: Fire breaks out in moving train’s engine in Lalmonirhat
item.description: Panic spread among passengers after a fire broke out in the engine of a moving commuter train at Hatibandha in Lalmonirhat on Sunday....

item.title: Parliamentary special committee holds first meeting on situation in energy sector
item.description: The first meeting of a special committee of the 13th national parliament on the country’s recent energy situation was held at the Jatiya Sangsad building on Sunday....

item.title: Trawler sinks in Meghna, 2 fishermen missing
item.description: An engine-operated wooden trawler sank in a tributary of the Meghna River at Hizla upazila in Barishal after being hit by a Nor’wester, leaving two fishermen missing....

item.title: Canal re-excavation work begins in Manikganj
item.description: Lawmaker for Manikganj 1 constituency SA Jinnah Kabir on Sunday inaugurated a canal re-excavation programme at Shibalaya upazila....

item.title: Chuadanga farmers rejoice over bumper boro yield
item.description: A BUMPER Boro paddy yield has brought relief and optimism to farmers across Chuadanga, as golden fields stretch across the district ahead of harvest, raising hopes for improved incomes despite rising production costs....

item.title: Govt to aid haor farmers via special cards: relief minister
item.description: Disaster Management and Relief minister Asadul Habib Dulu on Sunday said that the government has announced to stand by the farmers affected by the untimely floods in the haor areas....

item.title: Worshipper killed in mosque in Moulvibazar
item.description: A worshipper was stabbed to death by a youth inside a mosque during Fajr prayers at Shrimangal upazila in Moulvibazar on Sunday....

item.title: Man crushed under train in Mymensingh
item.description: An unidentified man was killed after being hit by a train while walking along railway tracks in Shambhuganj Railway Station area of Mymensingh on Sunday morning....

item.title: Govt for standardising Qoumi education stream
item.description: Education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Sunday said that they did not want the Qoumi education stream to remain separate....

item.title: Missing nine-year-old girl found dead after two days
item.description: A nine-year-old girl was found dead after being missing for two days at Akhaura in Brahmanbaria on Sunday at about 11:00am....

item.title: BMU begins workshop on teaching methodology
item.description: A three-day training workshop on ‘Teaching Methodology and Assessment for Faculty Members of BMU’ organised by the Institutional Quality Assurance Cell of Bangladesh Medical University began at its Shaheed Dr Milton Hall on Sunday....

item.title: Kazi Emdadul Hoque made new DG of BSTI
item.description: Retired tax commissioner of the National Board of Revenue, researcher and writer Kazi Emdadul Hoque has been appointed as director general of Bangladesh Standards and Testing Institution with the rank of secretary....Country specific content

item.title: Youth found hanging in Khulna
item.description: A young man was found hanging at his house in the Railway New Colony in Khulna on Sunday morning....

item.title: Krishak Dal leader’s body exhumed 14 months after burial in Lakshmipur
item.description: The body of a local Krishak Dal leader was exhumed following a court order, around 14 months after his burial in Terbeki of Lakshmipur municipality on Sunday....

item.title: United efforts urged to rebuild public trust in media
item.description: Editors, journalists, policymakers, civil society representatives and development partners on Sunday urged for collaborative efforts of the government and all media stakeholders to rebuild public trust in the media....

item.title: 152 suspected extortionists detained in city in 48hrs
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Police in a special combing operations detained 58 listed and 94 unlisted suspected extortionists from the capital in the past 48 hours till midnight past Saturday following specific intelligence....Geographic Reference

item.title: Measles vaccine coverage reaches 81pc: health minister
item.description: Health minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain on Sunday said that measles vaccination coverage had so far reached 81 per cent of targeted children and was expected to reach full coverage soon as the country battles an ongoing outbreak....

item.title: Kyiv hits Russian oil sites
item.description: Ukraine said Sunday it had hit several Russian ships—a cruise missile carrier and three shadow fleet tankers—as both sides fired hundreds of drones in a spree that killed at least eight people....

item.title: Israel extends 2 Gaza flotilla activists’ detention
item.description: An Israeli court Sunday extended by two days the detention of two foreign activists from a Gaza-bound flotilla, who were brought to Israel for questioning, a rights group representing them said....

item.title: Two women suffocate on migrant boat to UK
item.description: Two young Sudanese women appear to have suffocated to death on Sunday while trying to reach Britain from northern France in a small boat crammed with more than 80 people including pregnant women and children, officials said....

item.title: CJ suspends online hearings
item.description: The chief justice on Sunday suspended virtual hearings in both the High Court Division and the Appellate Division of the Supreme Court on Wednesdays and Thursdays until further notice....

item.title: BNP follows AL’s path by ignoring referendum verdict: opposition leaders
item.description: Leaders of the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami-led opposition alliance on Sunday alleged that the Bangladesh Nationalist Party was following the path of the Awami League by ignoring the verdict of the referendum, held simultaneously with the national elections on February 12....

item.title: Journalists stage protests at SC for access to courtrooms
item.description: Journalists covering the Supreme Court beat in Dhaka on Sunday formed a human chain, with their mouths masked by black cloth, to protest the restrictions on media access to courtrooms....Local news updates

item.title: Stakeholder engagement sought to meet challenges
item.description: The government and publishers and editors of newspapers on Sunday emphasised engagement among the stakeholders in the sector to meet the challenges it has been facing. ...

item.title: OPEC+ raises output quotas, silent on UAE exit
item.description: Saudi Arabia, Russia and five other OPEC+ countries increased their oil production quota on Sunday in an expected move aimed at demonstrating continuity at the cartel after the shock withdrawal of the United Arab Emirates....
